---
title: "Pink Redstem (Ammannia latifolia)"
slug: "ammannia-latifolia"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/474761152/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Pink Redstem"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Magnoliopsida"
tax_order: "Myrtales"
tax_family: "Lythraceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Magnoliopsida", "Myrtales", "Lythraceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/474761152/medium.jpg" alt="Pink Redstem" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/marinamarquis">
marinamarquis</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/264315930">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Magnoliopsida
-   **Order:** Myrtales
-   **Family:** Lythraceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=131593)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common in Florida found in wet, disturbed areas, tidal marshes, and ditches.

## Natural history notes

A wetland plant characterized by its reddish stems, opposite leaves, and small pink to purple flowers that occur in clusters along the stems. In Florida, it is found in shallow water and along the edges of ponds, marshes, and ditches. It grows during the warmer months and is often associated with seasonally flooded habitats.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/131593-Ammannia-latifolia)
-   [Local profile](https://florida.plantatlas.usf.edu/plant/species/3617)
